# Plan: staleness-detection

Goal: Make record staleness self-announcing: derive freshness signals at fold time from events already in the log — mechanical events (file_touched/command_run) and task changes appended after the last session_ended, phases still active while every task is done, and last-session summaries clipped by the status block's budget — and surface them on every read surface (SessionStart context block, sofar status, get_state digest) so a resuming session knows WHAT to distrust before it acts on a stale next_action. Zero new event types, zero capture burden: the log already carries the signals; the engine just has to announce them.

Progress: 12/12 tasks done (100%)

## Phase 1 — fold-time freshness derivation [done] — 3/3 done

- [x] 1.1 Freshness counter in fold: track events appended after the last session_ended (file_touched, command_run, task_status_changed, note_added, decision_logged — any source incl. cli) and expose state.freshness = { events_since_writeback by kind, last_writeback_ts }; zero new event types
- [x] 1.2 Stale-active-phase detection: extract doctor's inline D-P11 check (all tasks done, phase not done) into a shared core derivation; doctor.ts consumes it — one detector, two surfaces
- [x] 1.3 Truncation-aware clip: shared.ts clip/clipBlock variants that report whether they cut — the summary-truncation signal for any budgeted section (budgets live in the template, so detection happens at the render seam)

## Phase 2 — surfacing on every read surface [done] — 4/4 done

- [x] 2.1 Staleness line in renderStatus (new per-section budget const): '⚠ next action may be stale: N events since write-back (files/commands/tasks breakdown)' — rendered only when freshness reports drift; the shared seam covers BOTH the SessionStart block and get_state digest (get-state.ts delegates to renderStatus)
- [x] 2.2 Stale-active-phase marker on phase lines in renderStatus + renderFullStatus: budget-safe suffix like '[active — all tasks done; mark phase done?]', reusing the 1.2 shared detector
- [x] 2.3 renderFullStatus (sofar status): uncapped staleness section — event breakdown since write-back, stale phases, clipped-summary pointer (terminal surface, no 10k cap)
- [x] 2.4 Clipped-summary pointer: when 1.3 reports the last-session summary was cut in renderStatus, append '(clipped — full text in sessions/<id>.md)' inside SESSION_SUMMARY_BUDGET

## Phase 3 — SPEC + acceptance criteria [done] — 2/2 done

- [x] 3.1 SPEC §State: document the freshness derivation (events_since_writeback shape, D-P11 detector reuse, zero-new-event-types constraint); §MCP tools + §Hooks: the staleness line in digest and SessionStart block
- [x] 3.2 SPEC §Acceptance: staleness criteria — a stale log renders the warning and a fresh log renders none; 10k SessionStart cap holds; doctor stale-phase parity preserved after extraction

## Phase 4 — tests [done] — 3/3 done

- [x] 4.1 Fold tests: freshness over synthetic logs — events after session_ended counted by kind (incl. source cli), counter resets on a new write-back, replay deterministic (same log → deep-equal state incl. freshness)
- [x] 4.2 Render tests: staleness line present on stale state and absent on fresh; new budget respected; enforceStatusLimit ≤10k with all sections at worst case; clipped-summary pointer fires only when actually clipped
- [x] 4.3 Doctor regression: stale-phase WARN behavior byte-identical after the extraction to core (Phase 11 acceptance still passes)

Next action: None — initiative complete and closed.
